description:
Batch 1/5 — Core Pool Infrastructure (3 of 3)

Create WorkQueue struct that determines what the pool should run next:

- next_slot() -> Option<AgentSlot>: evaluates running workflows needing agent execution + ready tasks needing workflow creation, returns the highest priority one
- Priority order: (1) EM work queue pending entries, (2) running workflows with no active agent on current phase, (3) ready tasks by priority score
- Respects dependency gates — skips tasks with unmet blocked-by dependencies
- Deduplicates: won't return a task/workflow that already has an active agent
- Needs access to ServiceHub for task/workflow state and runner health for active agent tracking

This replaces the logic currently split between run_ready_task_workflows_for_project (lines 940-1095) and the workflow iteration in execute_running_workflow_phases_for_project (lines 1115-1209). Pure extraction + unification — same selection logic, single entry point.
title: Create work queue that feeds the agent pool with priority ordering
